answer:
Cloudflare supports two primary authentication methods for its API. The recommended approach is using an API Token via the Authorization: Bearer header. Alternatively, some legacy or specific endpoints support Global API Key authentication using X-Auth-Email and X-Auth-Key headers.

For API Token authentication, provide the token in your request headers:

```bash
curl "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/user/tokens/verify" \
--header "Authorization: Bearer <API_TOKEN>"
```

For Global API Key authentication, provide your email and key:

```bash
curl "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/user" \
--header "X-Auth-Email: <EMAIL>" \
--header "X-Auth-Key: <API_KEY>"
```

API Tokens are preferred for security as they support granular permissions and scoping. You should never store these credentials in plaintext or public repositories; use environment variables instead. Always use the latest version of the API, which is Version 4.
